LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS ZELKO SIMONI AND RENEE SIMONI'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS ESTABLISH IMMINENT AND IRREPARABLE HARM SINCE CONSTRUCTION WILL DESTROY THE CURRENT EASEMENTS. PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS WITH EVIDENCE OF AN EASEMENT BY IMPLICATION. FORMER OWNERS STATE EVERYONE USED THE EASEMENTS AND ALL OWNERS HAD KEYS. THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES AND HARMS WEIGH IN PLAINTIFFS' FAVOR. WITHOUT AN INJUNCTION THE STATUS QUO WILL CHANGE. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT ANYTHING SUBSTANTIVE IS HAPPENING ON THE CONSTRUCTION PROJECT. PLAINTIFFS TO POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $10,000 PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 529. ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND ORDER RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-19-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Plaintiffs Zelko Simoni And Renee Simoni'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 10/19/09)
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiffs Zelko Simoni and Renee Simoni's Order to Show Cause re: preliminary injunction. Appearances: Brenda Cruz Keith, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 626-6494 appearing Plaintiffs and moving party; Jay T. Jambeck, Esq. of the Schinner Law Group (415) 369-9050 appearing for Defendants Siavash Tahbazof and SYTS Investments, LLC and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Preliminary injunction granted. Plaintiffs establish imminent and irreparable harm since construction will destroy the current easements. Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the merits with evidence of an easement by implication. Former owners state everyone used the easements and all owners had keys. The balance of equities and harms weigh in plaintiffs' favor. Without an injunction the status quo will change. It does not appear that anything substantive is happening on the construction project. Plaintiffs to post a bond in the amount of $10,000 pursuant to CCP section 529. Order granting Motion for Preliminary Injunction and Order re: Preliminary Injunction signed in open Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
